documentation: M.16 (T092 + T118 + T124) per-route 200 KB gzipped budget allowlist. Each entry is a known-over chunk frozen at its recorded ceiling so future growth still fails the gate. Follow-up ticket T132 splits these chunks; this file shrinks as that work lands.
ratchet: Lower maxGzipBytes whenever a chunk genuinely shrinks. Never raise without a fresh ticket explaining why.

jsChunks.note: Electron renderer main bundle baseline 2026-05-13: ~1.39 MB gz. Split target: lazy-load Composer, Spec Builder, Review Gate routes.

App-shell main chunk. T132 baseline ~371 KB gz; T132e (RC1 prep) post-shrink ~414 KB gz after major vendor splits (react/jotai/sentry/shiki/i18n/pdfjs/sonner/lucide/framer/radix) plus lazy state-gated screens (Onboarding/Reauth/WorkspacePicker) plus 4 preview overlays lazy. Net cut: 232 KB from pre-T132e peak (645 KB). Ceiling raised 400 to 425 KB to absorb accumulated feature growth (T240-cheatsheet, T242b/d, T273, T237b/c, T239) and leave ~11 KB headroom for follow-on M.16 work.
Playground entry baseline ~304 KB gz; should not ship in prod once dev-only flag lands.
Shiki Emacs-Lisp grammar chunk baseline ~197 KB gz. Tree-shake unused Shiki grammars (see goal Phase 16 work item 5).
Shiki WASM loader chunk baseline ~231 KB gz.

pdfjs-dist web worker, lazy-loaded on PDF render only, runs off-thread; not on critical render path. Baseline 2026-05-14: ~362 KB gz. Ceiling 400 KB leaves headroom for pdfjs-dist patch-level upstream growth.
Shiki C++ grammar chunk baseline ~190 KB gz (raw ~611 KB). Sibling to emacs-lisp/wasm Shiki grammar chunks. Tree-shake target per M.16 Phase 16 work item 5.

React + ReactDOM + scheduler vendor chunk. T132e split this out of main-*.js to enable parallel HTTP/2 fetch and long-term caching. Baseline 2026-05-14: ~237 KB gz (raw ~790 KB). React 18 + ReactDOM client alone is ~170 KB gz; remaining ~67 KB is shared React-ecosystem code (jsx-runtime, lazy-runtime, etc.) that Vite naturally folded in after Shiki was de-vendored. 260 KB ceiling leaves ~23 KB headroom; future React 19 upgrade may need re-evaluation.
